user.title: SMA Inverters – Global Settings
user.description: Configure how often Home Assistant should poll your SMA inverter(s). You can add more inverters after the initial setup via the Options menu.

data_description.scan_interval: How frequently (in seconds) the integration reads data from each inverter via Modbus TCP. Minimum 10 s.

inverter.title: SMA Inverters – Add Inverter
inverter.description: Enter the Modbus TCP connection details for your SMA inverter. The integration will test the connection before saving.

data_description.name: A friendly name shown in the device registry (e.g. 'Rooftop South').
data_description.section: Optional grouping label (e.g. 'Building A'). Used to uniquely identify the device.
data_description.host: IP address or hostname of the inverter's Modbus TCP interface.
data_description.port: Modbus TCP port. SMA default is 502.
data_description.slave_id: Modbus unit / slave ID. SMA default is 3.

error.connection: Could not connect to the inverter. Check the host, port, and that the inverter is powered on and reachable.
error.communication: Connected but failed to read data. Check the slave ID and that Modbus TCP is enabled on the inverter.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: This inverter entry is already configured.

data_description.scan_interval: How frequently (in seconds) the integration reads data from each inverter. Changes take effect after saving.
